codeReview.summary: The feature is close to the requested workflow, but it has two blocking issues: a hardcoded deployer private key in source code and incorrect USD1 orderbook balance reporting caused by USDC-decimal scaling.

issues.description: A deployer private key is hardcoded as DEFAULT_DEPLOYER_KEY and used as fallback (line 96). This violates project security policy (AGENTS.md line 79: no hardcoded private keys) and can leak privileged credentials.

USD1 orderbook balance is read from balances.usdc.available and printed as USD1. SDK getBalances scales quote balances by USDC_DECIMALS=6 (sdk/src/client.ts lines 397-399), but USD1 uses 8 decimals, so reported USD1 orderbook/total balances are incorrect.

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker skill is API/service-centric and does not explicitly require running new blockchain scripts as part of verification, which can miss feature requirements like 'script must actually RUN'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md lines 28-51 focus on tests + starting API service; handoff verification only ran pnpm -r typecheck and pnpm -r test (.factory/missions/79476079-9b4c-40cb-b69e-9e85801c6056/handoffs/2026-04-01T06-28-07-968Z__demo-wallet-setup-script__edc3f53c-3a4d-4eed-87b1-3602d2a254e8.json lines 14-23).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture states quote decimals are configurable (6 or 8), but SDK getBalances remains USDC-shaped/USDC-decimal scaled. This hidden limitation should be documented in library until fixed to prevent misuse in scripts.

summary: Review failed: the commit introduces a hardcoded private key and misreports USD1 orderbook balances due to USDC decimal scaling. I reviewed the transcript skeleton, handoff, skill file, and full commit diff.
